sourceRefs.summary: Fig. 7 maps to temporal expression, Setd1b cKO phenotype, and RFX2-axis claims.
Fig. 7 legend plus RFX2/SETD1B result text.

inputRefs.summary: S8 early and late expressed gene sets with expression and H3K4me3 values across spermatogenic stages.
S1 confirms Setd1bKO RNA-seq sample labels, but contains no Rfx2 sample labels in the local workbook.

codeRefs.summary: Parsed S8 and summarized per-gene peak-stage distributions and median expression/H3K4me3 values.
Parsed S1 sample labels for perturbation evidence boundaries.

outputFigureRefs.summary: S8 supports temporal expression patterns for early and late gene sets at table level.
RFX2 perturbation remains insufficient from local tables and PDF text alone.

statisticsRefs.summary: Early genes: 515 genes, peak stages RS2=205 and RS4=116; late genes: 506 genes, peak stage RS10=475.
Gene length and stage-composition confounding remain unresolved.

stdoutRefs.summary: Local parse stdout includes S8 peak-stage and median summaries.

stderrRefs.summary: No stderr from bounded local supplement parse.

limitations: S8 supports temporal gene-set behavior but is itself a published supplement, not an independent RNA-seq reanalysis.
Setd1b perturbation timing cannot be reproduced without control/cKO count matrices or processed outputs for RS4, RS8, and LS.
Rfx2 KO broad-domain reduction and RFX2-SETD1B interaction are not reproduced from local tables; only PDF text reports them.
Phenotype panels are not reproduced because local quantitative phenotype tables/images were not in the selected supplement subset.
diagnostics: Structured partial: temporal early/late gene-set patterns are checkable and directionally supported by S8.
Structured insufficient-evidence: Setd1b/Rfx2 perturbation causality and phenotype panels require additional processed/raw data.
